news.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina 3-1 Switzerland (AET): Julian Alvarez scores stunner to set up blockbuster semi-final vs England
news.lede: Julian Alvarez scored an extra-time stunner as Argentina beat 10-man Switzerland 3-1 and set up a mouth-watering World Cup semi-final clash with England. Lionel Messi and Co will face the Three Lions in Atlanta on Wednesday at 8pm UK time, with a place in the final against either France or Spain on the line. It will be the first competitive meeting between the nations in 24 years. Alexis Mac Allister was twice left unmarked at a corner - and the second time he punished them, by guiding in a header in after being picked out by Messi.

news.title: World Cup 2026: Which teams have travelled the furthest?
news.lede: England have been staying at Swope Soccer Village in Kansas City. A World Cup staged across three countries was always going to test teams in ways no previous tournament had. With 16 host cities spread across North America, nations have travelled further than before in pursuit of football's biggest prize. But while every team has had to contend with the vast distances, some have covered considerably more ground than others.

news.title: World Cup 2026: Norway 1-2 England (AET) - Jude Bellingham the hero again as Three Lions advance to semi-finals
news.lede: England booked their place in the semi-finals of the World Cup as another double from the brilliant Jude Bellingham clinched a 2-1 extra-time victory over Norway in Miami. Five days after his two-goal heroics against Mexico, Jude Bellingham was England's saviour once again, cancelling out Andreas Schjelderup's opener with a superb finish in first-half stoppage time before grabbing the extra-time winner from close range. The goals take the 23-year-old to six at the tournament, putting him level with Harry Kane and only one behind Erling Haaland, who struggled to make an impact for Norway in a gruelling contest impacted by the hot and humid Florida conditions.

news.title: How France and Spain’s World Cup clash of contrasts could decide football’s tactical future
news.lede: Much more than just attack against defence, the distinct identities of the two sides could create a compelling contest in Dallas. From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: France at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about France at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. France were runners-up last time and will have an eye on the gold medals at FIFA World Cup 2026. Didier Deschamps' side, led by captain Kylian Mbappé, topped Group I ahead of Norway, Senegal and Iraq to reach the knockout phase. They then beat Sweden in the round of 32, Paraguay in the last 16 and Morocco in the quarter-finals.

news.title: 2026 World Cup results, standings and schedule: Live scores, knockout round updates and how to watch
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has reached the semifinals with both matchups now set following the conclusion of the quarterfinals. Reigning champions Argentina will take on England in Atlanta, while France will face Spain in Dallas. The three tournament co-hosts were each knocked out in the Round of 16 after Canada lostMorocco, Mexico fell to England and the USMNT was eliminated by Belgium. Below is a complete look at every group's final standings, which teams advanced, which were eliminated and the full knockout round schedule.

news.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 faces Cybersecurity concerns amid mass Email Bomb Campaigns
news.lede: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has not only been marked by intense competition on the football field but has also become the center of growing controversies involving cyber threats, misinformation, and digital manipulation. As the tournament progresses into its crucial stages, several incidents have raised concerns about how cybersecurity challenges are increasingly intersecting with major global sporting events. Over the past few days, tensions among participating football nations have escalated significantly. Allegations of unfair influence, controversial officiating decisions, and the spread of misleading online content have dominated discussions alongside the matches themselves.

news.title: The 2026 North American World Cup is rocking the 'football barren' U.S. with huge ratings, dramatic
news.lede: Axios reported on the 12th (local time) that experts feared that the World Cup could be shunned in a polarized, exclusive, and inflationary U.S. society for months, but the World Cup set a record for the most viewers in U.S. soccer history, dispelling such skepticism at once. First of all, despite the fact that all three host countries have been eliminated, viewership, number of spectators, and buzz are sky-high. Now, fans are set to play England vs Argentina and France vs Spain in the 'Dream semifinals'. Despite the U.S. defeat by Belgium 4-1 in the round of 16, Fox and Telemundo combined attracted a whopping 50.1 million final viewers.
